questions.q: Where do we run log4j 2.14 today?
Is this release cleared to ship?
What exactly is deployed in production right now?
Which of our code did agents write, and under what approval?

findBlocks.title: One structure, six levels, zero stitching
findBlocks.body: Component releases bundle into product releases as they are produced. No manual assembly, no stale spreadsheets. The hierarchy above is not a diagram of your process; it is the live data model. Multi-level nesting is supported: products can contain products, and evidence propagates through every level.
findBlocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT 4: hierarchy / product release composition view ]

findBlocks.title: Product-level count to exact SBOM entry in three clicks
findBlocks.body: Start from a product-level vulnerability count. Click to the offending component, then to the exact SBOM entry that carries the finding. Every number on the dashboard is traversable to its source. Findings of every type are covered: vulnerabilities, weaknesses, and license violations, aggregated from Dependency-Track and your other tools, with triage and audit at organization, product, component or release scope.
findBlocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT: drill-down from product finding to SBOM entry ]

findBlocks.title: What did we know at ship time vs what do we know now
findBlocks.body: New CVEs and KEV entries are evaluated against shipped versions, not just the latest build. Posture at ship time is preserved as evidence; current posture is computed continuously.
findBlocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT: shipped-version posture vs current posture ]

prodPages.label: SBOM/XBOM Management
prodPages.problem: SBOMs generated in CI end up as build artifacts nobody can query. ReARM attaches every SBOM and XBOM to the release that produced it, per component and per product.

blocks.title: Not a bucket of JSON files
blocks.body: Every SBOM and XBOM is versioned and tied to the release that produced it, per component and per product. Ask for any shipped version's bill of materials and get it in one click.

blocks.title: What changed between 1.2 and 1.3? Two answers, same release pair.
blocks.body: Dependency delta: every new release is automatically diffed against its predecessor as it lands. Continuous, not on-request; any two versions diffable on demand, at component and product level. Code delta: auto-generated changelogs from the commits between the two releases, shown alongside the SBOM diff. Releases are versioned automatically per your org's version schema; no manual bumping. Finding delta: how vulnerabilities and policy findings changed between the two releases, in the same changelog view.
blocks.callout: Dependency-analysis tools do not do cross-version SBOM diffing. ReARM does.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT 1 + 3: SBOM diff between versions, changelog alongside ]

blocks.title: Where do we run log4j 2.14?
blocks.body: Answered portfolio-wide in seconds, across every product and every shipped version. Not just the latest build: everything you have ever released.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT 2: dependency search results across portfolio ]

blocks.title: HBOMs, VDRs, attestations, anything
blocks.body: HBOMs, VEX, VDR, BOV, SARIF, digital signatures, attestations, build metadata: every artifact needed to prove integrity, safety, and compliance, attached to the release that produced it, versioned, and retained for 10+ years. ReARM is the system of record for your supply chain evidence.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT: artifacts attached to a release ]

blocks.title: Serve SBOMs downstream
blocks.body: Distribute SBOMs and artifacts to downstream consumers via TEA. Customers, authorities, and program offices pull from the same organized source.

blocks.title: SBOMs arrive incomplete. BEAR fixes that.
blocks.body: Reliza BEAR, ReARM's agentic SBOM enrichment and augmentation tool, automatically enriches your SBOMs with additional metadata, including supplier, copyright and license information, before they roll up.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT: BEAR-enriched SBOM entry, before and after ]

prodPages.label: Release Policies
prodPages.h1: Encode your release rules once. Every release is checked, with evidence.
prodPages.problem: Release criteria that live in wiki pages and tribal knowledge do not get enforced. ReARM turns them into policies that run against every release, automatically.

blocks.title: Draft, assembled, approved or rejected
blocks.body: Approval policies and a release lifecycle enforced automatically on every release. No release reaches approved without passing the rules your organization set. Both manual and automated approvals are supported.

blocks.title: Reject release if critical or high vulnerabilities > 0
blocks.body: That is an actual policy rule, and this is an actual release it rejected. Not a mockup: the screenshot shows the rule and the REJECTED release side by side.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT 5: rejected release + the policy rule that rejected it ]

blocks.title: What a blocked release looks like
blocks.body: A failed check on the pipeline, an annotation on the pull request, a release that cannot proceed. Rejection is visible where engineers work, not buried in a dashboard.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT 6: PR check / annotation from a rejected release ]

blocks.title: Who approved, when, against what
blocks.body: Every approval records who or what approved, when, and against which evidence snapshot. Years later, the answer is still there. Every approval is immutably recorded with a timestamp, approver identity, and the evidence it was based on. Your release approval workflow is also your audit trail.

prodPages.label: AI Governance
prodPages.h1: Know exactly what your agents shipped.
prodPages.problem: Coding agents multiply commit volume. 'Who wrote this, and under what approval' stops being answerable by convention. ReARM makes it answerable by record.

blocks.title: Per-commit attribution, cryptographically signed
blocks.body: Agent vs human, which agent, which model. Backed by signed commits, not commit-message convention.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT 7: signed agent commit with trailers ]

blocks.title: Scoped, auditable work sessions
blocks.body: Each agent works in a session with orientation and final reports attached as evidence. What it was asked to do and what it says it did, on the record.

blocks.title: The same policy engine that gates humans
blocks.body: Approval policies keyed on agent and session state. Agent-written code passes the same gates as human-written code, or it does not ship.
blocks.shot: [ SCREENSHOT: policy gate on agent work ]

ucPages.label: Medical Devices
ucPages.h1: Submission-ready SBOMs. Audit-ready evidence. For every device release.
ucPages.pain: FDA section 524B premarket cybersecurity requires SBOMs and ongoing vulnerability monitoring. Auditors ask for evidence years after ship. Build records scattered across CI systems do not survive that question.
ucPages.evidence: SBOM · VDR · KEV MONITORING · RELEASE CRITERIA · IMMUTABLE HISTORY

steps.title: Submission-ready SBOMs
steps.body: Generated and stored per device release, versioned and tied to the release that produced them.

steps.title: Postmarket monitoring
steps.body: New CVEs and KEV entries evaluated against shipped versions, not just the latest build.

steps.title: Documented, enforced release criteria
steps.body: Release policies encode your criteria once; every release is checked automatically, with evidence.

steps.title: Immutable release history
steps.body: A record of what actually shipped, holding up years later when auditors ask.

ucPages.label: Field Digital Twin
ucPages.h1: A digital twin of every fielded unit: hardware, software, and every replacement.
ucPages.pain: Fielded configurations drift from as-built the moment a part is swapped or firmware is patched. 'Which fielded units carry X?' is unanswerable from build records alone.
ucPages.evidence: PER-UNIT CONFIGURATION · DISTRIBUTION MODEL · SUSTAINMENT AUDITS
ucPages.note: copy pending verification against actual distribution page

steps.title: HBOM + SBOM per unit
steps.body: Hardware and software bill of materials per unit and per configuration, kept together.

steps.title: Per-deployment composition
steps.body: Each fielded deployment's exact composition tracked via the distribution model.

steps.title: Replacements tracking
steps.body: Part and component swaps recorded against the twin. As-maintained, not just as-built.

steps.title: Fleet-wide exposure queries
steps.body: By vulnerability or by part number: which fielded units carry X, answered in seconds.

steps.title: Evidence trail for sustainment audits
steps.body: The same immutable history, extended to every fielded unit's service life.

ucPages.label: Deployment Governance
ucPages.h1: What should be running, what is actually running, and the delta.
ucPages.pain: Release records say what was approved. Nobody can prove what is deployed where right now. ReARM closes the loop between plan and actual.
ucPages.evidence: INSTANCES · ENVIRONMENTS · FEATURE-SET TARGETING · RUNTIME EVIDENCE
ucPages.shot: [ SCREENSHOT 8: instances page, plan vs actual side by side ]

steps.title: Instances and environments registered
steps.body: Every instance and environment is a first-class object in ReARM, not a spreadsheet row.

steps.title: Plan vs actual, continuously reconciled
steps.body: Approved and expected state vs observed state, resolved side by side and kept current.

steps.title: Drift surfaced immediately
steps.body: The moment actual diverges from plan, it is visible. No quarterly audits to find out.

steps.title: Feature-set targeting
steps.body: Point an instance at a feature set; ReARM reconciles what should land there.

steps.title: Evidence, extended to runtime
steps.body: The same evidence trail that covers releases now covers what actually runs.

ucPages.label: EU CRA Compliance
ucPages.persona: FOR ENGINEERING + COMPLIANCE AT MANUFACTURERS SELLING INTO THE EU
ucPages.pain: The Cyber Resilience Act makes SBOMs, coordinated vulnerability handling, and due-diligence evidence a condition of selling into the EU. ReARM turns each obligation into something your pipeline already does.
ucPages.evidence: ANNEX I · SBOM · CVD · DISTRIBUTION (TEA) · DUE-DILIGENCE EVIDENCE

steps.title: SBOM per product release
steps.body: Annex I requirements met by default: every product release carries its SBOM.

steps.title: Coordinated vulnerability handling
steps.body: Findings triage and analysis workflow, documented as it happens.

steps.title: Distribution to authorities and customers
steps.body: SBOMs and artifacts served downstream via TEA.

steps.title: Due-diligence evidence
steps.body: Documented release criteria and immutable history, ready when the question comes.
